caveat: Balanced accuracy averages sensitivity and specificity so the negative class cannot dominate the score.
interpretation: This is a fairer accuracy-style score for imbalanced diabetes prediction.

caveat: For Brier score, lower is better because it measures probability error.
interpretation: This shows the best probability-estimation slice among the current runs.

caveat: For ECE, lower is better. A very small value means probability estimates match observed rates well.
interpretation: This is a probability-quality score, not a classification accuracy score.

caveat: F1 balances precision and recall, so it is usually more informative than raw accuracy under imbalance.
interpretation: This is the strongest decision threshold for catching positives without ignoring false alarms.

caveat: Overall accuracy is useful, but can look high on imbalanced data because the majority class can dominate.
interpretation: This is the highest legitimate thresholded accuracy found in the existing sweep.

caveat: PR AUC is usually harder than ROC AUC on imbalanced diabetes data.
interpretation: This is the best precision-recall ranking result across the saved runs.

caveat: ROC AUC is threshold-independent ranking quality, not percent-correct accuracy.
interpretation: This is the best ranking result across the saved current-dataset runs.

caveat: This specificity is computed from the saved selected-threshold confusion matrix.
interpretation: It confirms whether the deployed threshold avoids false positives for non-diabetic cases.

caveat: Near-99 overlap means clients emphasize similar top features; it is not prediction accuracy.
interpretation: This shows whether explanations agree across federated clients.

caveat: Near-99 stability means feature rankings are consistent; it is not prediction accuracy.
interpretation: This supports the interpretability part of the trustworthiness claim.

caveat: High precision can occur at strict thresholds that predict fewer positives.
interpretation: Use this to understand how confident positive predictions can be at a chosen threshold.

caveat: Near-99 recall means most diabetic cases are caught, but it can create many false positives.
interpretation: Use this as a safety-oriented operating view, not as overall accuracy.

caveat: High specificity means many non-diabetic cases are correctly left negative; it does not mean diabetic recall is high.
interpretation: Use this as a low-false-alarm operating view, not as overall accuracy.

caveat: This specificity is computed from the saved selected-threshold confusion matrix.
interpretation: It confirms whether the deployed threshold avoids false positives for non-diabetic cases.
